description: VOL XL PINE BLUFF ARKANSAS WEDNESDAY MORNING MAY 4 1898 NO 81 THE CABLE CUT And Nothing Further Has Been Heard 9 From Manilla i 8cripp6McRae Telegram LONDON May 3A Madrid dispatch dated at 3 oclock this morning says nothing further has been received from Manilla The cable is unquestionably cut Latest advices received at the Colonial office say outlying native districts of Manilla suffered some f what from the naval engagement and a number of them were burned A European town was struck by shells The citadel batteries in flicted considerable damage on the bombarders It is stated that the Spanish naval authorities have determined to avoid isolated com bats but with united naval strength will endeavor to crush the American squadron in Cuban waters A dispatch from Cadiz says the Paloyo Alphonso XIII and Victoria are nearly ready to sail Private advices say Spain is experiencing great difficulty owing to lack of money British engineers refuse to serve aboard Spanish vessels Passengers from Cuba NEW YORK May 3The British steamer Ely arrived this morning from Guantanmo Cuba She had 80 passengers including U S Consul Trice Baracoa Before leaving the Captain was in formed by the British Consul
